note: Genuine apples-to-apples ALM comparison (both are audio LMs, not TTS/ASR). The 200M Sanskrit-specialist Śabda-ALM (CER 0.565) vastly outperforms the 7B general-purpose Qwen2-Audio (CER 15.9), which cannot transcribe Sanskrit — it ignores the audio and returns a canned hallucination. Per-item predictions in alm_vs_alm_records.json.

qwen_evidence.finding: Qwen2-Audio-7B genuinely ran (0 errors) but emitted ONE identical hallucination for all 58 clips — it does not condition on the Sanskrit audio at all. CER is high (15.9) because that fixed output is ~3x the length of the gold and unrelated to it.
